cases.description: Single text field — basic string encoding
Integer field — value 1
Integer field — value 23
Integer field — value 24
Integer field — value 256
Negative integer — value -1
Boolean fields — true and false
Timestamp field — ISO 8601 string stays as string (no numeric conversion in RFC 8785)
cases.notes: RFC 8785 has no schema-aware type coercion; ISO 8601 strings stay as-is

cases.description: Timestamp field — non-midnight time
Date field — YYYY-MM-DD string stays as string
Binary field — base64url string stays as string (no decode in RFC 8785)
cases.notes: base64url strings are opaque JSON strings; RFC 8785 does not distinguish them from other strings

cases.description: Binary field — longer base64url string stays as string
Map key ordering — keys sorted lexicographically by Unicode code point; "a" < "b" < "c"
cases.notes: RFC 8785 §3.2.3: keys sorted by Unicode code-point order (standard JS string sort)

cases.description: Map key ordering — "a" sorts before "bb" because "a" < "bb" lexicographically
cases.notes: Unlike CBOR (§4.2.1), RFC 8785 uses pure Unicode code-point order, not length-first

cases.description: Nested map — inner map keys are also sorted
Optional field omission — null value is stripped; result is identical to TC-01
cases.notes: in_reply_to is null → omitted from output

cases.description: Unicode text field — UTF-8 multi-byte character
cases.notes: U+00E9 (é) serialized as-is per RFC 8785 / ECMAScript JSON.stringify; only control chars and '"' '\' are escaped

cases.description: Array field — array of text strings
Array of binary fields — base64url strings stay as strings
Full message envelope payload — text, array, timestamp, and binary fields together
Log entry with version, prev_log_root, and field update
Revocation entry — code (integer), effective_date (timestamp string), note (text)
CardDocument issuer-signature input — fields present at offer time before the offerer signs; ancestry_pubkeys populated with two entries
cases.notes: Issuer-signature input: recipient_pubkey, holder_signature, press_signature are absent (not yet added); issuer_signature is absent (it is the output of this step). Key order: ancestry_pubkeys < issued_at < issuer_card < policy_id < press_card. ancestry_pubkeys sorts first because 'a' (U+0061) < every other initial letter here.

cases.description: CardDocument holder-signature input — issuer_signature and recipient_pubkey added; holder_signature and press_signature still absent
cases.notes: Holder-signature input: adds issuer_signature and recipient_pubkey to TC-23; excludes holder_signature and press_signature. Key order: ancestry_pubkeys < issued_at < issuer_card < issuer_signature < policy_id < press_card < recipient_pubkey. Note issuer_card < issuer_signature because 'd' (U+0064) < 's' (U+0073) at position 7.

cases.description: CardDocument press-signature input — holder_signature added; press_signature still absent; this is the byte sequence the press signs
cases.notes: Press-signature input: all fields except press_signature. Key order: ancestry_pubkeys < holder_signature < issued_at < issuer_card < issuer_signature < policy_id < press_card < recipient_pubkey. holder_signature ('h') < issued_at ('i') < issuer_card ('i','s','s','u','e','r','_','c') < issuer_signature ('i','s','s','u','e','r','_','s').

cases.description: CardDocument with ancestry_pubkeys: [] — root base case (INC-41); complete stored document with all five signature fields present
cases.notes: Pins that ancestry_pubkeys: [] serializes as the two-character token '[]' — a present empty array, NOT field omission (which would remove the key entirely per A.3). This is the INC-41 normative requirement for trusted-root / root-parent cards. All nine mandatory CardDocument fields present; key order: ancestry_pubkeys < holder_signature < issued_at < issuer_card < issuer_signature < policy_id < press_card < press_signature < recipient_pubkey.
